revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{神社| 名称=須々岐水神社| 所在地=長野県松本市里山辺薄町| 例祭=5月4日、5日}} '''須々岐水神社'''（すすきがわじんじ...'
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=須々岐水神社| 所在地=長野県松本市里山辺薄町| 例祭=5月4日、5日}} '''須々岐水神社'''（すすきがわじんじゃ）は[[長野県]][[松本市]][[里山辺村|里山辺]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 歴史 == * [[867年]]（[[貞観]]9年）に、安曇大野川の梓水神社とともに、[[正六位上]]から[[従五位下]]に昇叙されたと、『日本三代実録』に記録が残る。 * [[1458年]]（[[長禄]]2年）の墨書銘をもつ[[狛犬]]が残る。 * [[信府統記]]の「松本領諸社記」には、「薄宮大明神」として「山奥の大明神平に降りた神が、笹の葉に乗って[[薄川]]をくだり薄畑に着き、現在の社地に移った」との伝承が記録されている。
== 境内 == [[境内]]の「桜之宮」には[[聖徳太子]]が祀られている。
== お船祭り == 5月5日・6日に行われる例祭である。9の町会がもっている「お船」（一般には[[山車]]（だし）と呼ばれ、曳き山・屋台・舞台などと呼称することもあるが、須々岐水神社では「お船」と呼ぶ）を境内まで曳く。9基のお船には彫刻が施されており、いずれも県宝に指定されている。
== 御柱祭り == 6年に1回、兎年と酉年に行われる。市の重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている。
== 参考文献 == * 『松本まるごと博物館 ガイドブック』松本市立博物館、2008年3月
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すすきかわしんしや}} [[Category:松本市の神社]]

{{神社| 名称=須々岐水神社| 所在地=長野県松本市里山辺薄町| 例祭=5月4日、5日}} '''須々岐水神社'''（すすきがわじんじゃ）は[[長野県]][[松本市]][[里山辺村|里山辺]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 歴史 == * [[867年]]（[[貞観]]9年）に、安曇大野川の梓水神社とともに、[[正六位上]]から[[従五位下]]に昇叙されたと、『日本三代実録』に記録が残る。 * [[1458年]]（[[長禄]]2年）の墨書銘をもつ[[狛犬]]が残る。 * [[信府統記]]の「松本領諸社記」には、「薄宮大明神」として「山奥の大明神平に降りた神が、笹の葉に乗って[[薄川]]をくだり薄畑に着き、現在の社地に移った」との伝承が記録されている。
== 境内 == [[境内]]の「桜之宮」には[[聖徳太子]]が祀られている。
== お船祭り == 5月5日・6日に行われる例祭である。9の町会がもっている「お船」（一般には[[山車]]（だし）と呼ばれ、曳き山・屋台・舞台などと呼称することもあるが、須々岐水神社では「お船」と呼ぶ）を境内まで曳く。9基のお船には彫刻が施されており、いずれも県宝に指定されている。[[「お船祭り (須々岐水神社)」|お船祭り]]については、別途詳しく項目が立てられているので、そちらを参照されたい。
== 御柱祭り == 6年に1回、兎年と酉年に行われる。市の重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている。
== 参考文献 == * 『松本まるごと博物館 ガイドブック』松本市立博物館、2008年3月
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すすきかわしんしや}} [[Category:松本市の神社]]

== 境内 == [[境内]]の「桜之宮」には[[聖徳太子]]が祀られている。
== お船祭り == 5月5日・6日に行われる例祭である。9の町会がもっている「お船」（一般には[[山車]]（だし）と呼ばれ、曳き山・屋台・舞台などと呼称することもあるが、須々岐水神社では「お船」と呼ぶ）を境内まで曳く。9基のお船には彫刻が施されており、いずれも県宝に指定されている。「[[お船祭り (須々岐水神社)|お船祭り]]」については、別途詳しく項目が立てられているので、そちらを参照されたい。
== 御柱祭り == 6年に1回、兎年と酉年に行われる。市の重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている。
== 参考文献 == * 『松本まるごと博物館 ガイドブック』松本市立博物館、2008年3月

{{神社| 名称=須々岐水神社| 画像={{画像募集中|cat=松本市}}| 所在地=長野県松本市里山辺薄町| 例祭=5月5日}} '''須々岐水神社'''（すすきがわじんじゃ）は[[長野県]][[松本市]][[里山辺村|里山辺]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 歴史 == * [[867年]]（[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]9年）に、安曇大野川の梓水神社とともに、[[正六位上]]から[[従五位下]]に昇叙されたと、『日本三代実録』に記録が残る。 * [[1458年]]（[[長禄]]2年）の墨書銘をもつ[[狛犬]]が残る。 * [[信府統記]]の「松本領諸社記」には、「薄宮大明神」として「山奥の大明神平に降りた神が、笹の葉に乗って[[薄川]]をくだり薄畑に着き、現在の社地に移った」との伝承が記録されている。
== 境内 == [[境内]]の「桜之宮」には[[聖徳太子]]が祀られている。
== お船祭り == 5月5日に行われる例祭である。9つの町会がもっている「お船」（一般には[[山車]]（だし）と呼ばれ、曳き山・屋台・舞台などと呼称することもあるが、須々岐水神社では「お船」と呼ぶ）を境内まで曳く。9基のお船には彫刻が施されており、いずれも県宝に指定されている。「[[お船祭り (須々岐水神社)|お船祭り]]」については、別途詳しく項目が立てられているので、そちらを参照されたい。
== 御柱祭り == 6年に1回、[[卯]]年と[[酉]]年に行われる。市の重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている。
== 参考文献 == * 『松本まるごと博物館 ガイドブック』松本市立博物館、2008年3月
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すすきかわしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:松本市の建築物]] [[Category:松本市の歴史]]

== 境内 == [[境内]]の「桜之宮」には[[聖徳太子]]が祀られている。
== お船祭り == 5月5日に行われる例祭である。9つの町会がもっている「お船」（一般には[[山車]]（だし）と呼ばれ、曳き山・屋台・舞台などと呼称することもあるが、須々岐水神社では「お船」と呼ぶ）を境内まで曳く。9基のお船には彫刻が施されており、いずれも県宝に指定されている。「[[お船祭り (須々岐水神社)|お船祭り]]」については、別途詳しく項目が立てられているので、そちらを参照されたい。
== 御柱祭り == 6年に1回、[[卯]]年と[[酉]]年に行われる。市の重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている。
== 参考文献 == * 『松本まるごと博物館 ガイドブック』松本市立博物館、2008年3月

{{神社 |名称 = 須須岐水神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|250px]]
[[お船祭り (須々岐水神社)|お船祭り]]（[[2008年]][[5月5日]]） |所在地 = [[長野県]][[松本市]][[大字]][[里山辺村|里山辺]]2737長野県神社庁「[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm 神社紹介 松塩筑支部]」より（2016年1月24日閲覧）。座標は[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ ジオロケーター 日本語版]にて「須々岐水神社」をキーワードに検索して得た（2016年1月24日閲覧）。 | 緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 14 |緯度秒 = 0.94 | 経度度 = 138|経度分 = 0 |経度秒 = 27.6 |ラベル位置 = |地図 = Japan Nagano |祭神 = [[薄川]]の神・[[タケミナカタ|建御名方命]]・[[スサノオ|素盞鳴命]]信州・長野県観光協会「[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html 長野県公式観光ウェブサイト さわやか信州旅.net 須々岐水神社]」より（2016年1月24日閲覧）。 |神体 = |社格 = [[国史見在社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 須々岐水神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[5月5日]]（[[お船祭り (須々岐水神社)|お船祭り]]） |神事 = }} [[ファイル:Susuki River from Tsukamabashi Bridge.jpg|thumb|松本市を流れる[[薄川]]]] '''須々岐水神社'''（すすきがわじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[松本市]][[里山辺村|里山辺]]にある[[神社]]。[[登記]]上の[[宗教法人]]名称は'''須須岐水神社'''。 == 歴史 == * [[867年]]（[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]9年）に、[[大野川 (松本市)|安曇大野川]]の[[梓水神社]]とともに、[[正六位上]]から[[従五位下]]に昇叙されたと、『[[日本三代実録]]』に記録が残る。 * [[1458年]]（[[長禄]]2年）の墨書銘をもつ[[狛犬]]が残る。 * 『[[信府統記]]』の「松本領諸社記」には、「薄宮大明神」として「山奥の大明神平に降りた神が、笹の葉に乗って[[薄川]]をくだり薄畑に着き、現在の社地に移った」との伝承が記録されている。 == 境内 == [[境内]]の「桜之宮」には[[聖徳太子]]が祀られている。 == 例祭 == ;お船祭り :{{Main|お船祭り (須々岐水神社)}} :5月5日に行われる例祭である。9つの町会がもっている「お船」（一般には[[山車]]（だし）と呼ばれ、曳き山・屋台・舞台などと呼称することもあるが、須々岐水神社では「お船」と呼ぶ）を境内まで曳く。9基のお船には彫刻が施されており、いずれも長野県宝に指定されている。 ;御柱祭り :7年に1回、[[卯]]年と[[酉]]年に行われる。松本市の重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている。 == 交通アクセス == ;[[公共交通機関]] :[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[松本駅]]から[[タクシー]]で20[[分]]間。 ;[[自家用自動車]] :[[長野自動車道]]・[[松本インターチェンジ]]から7[[キロメートル]]、[[自動車]]で25分間。[[駐車場]]はない。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『松本まるごと博物館 ガイドブック』[[松本市立博物館]]、2008年3月 == 関連項目 == * [[須須岐水神社]]・[[お船祭り]] - 曖昧さ回避 * [[薄川]] * [[水神]] * [[里山辺村]] * [[御柱祭]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html 長野県公式観光ウェブサイト さわやか信州旅.net 須々岐水神社] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm 松本市公式観光情報ポータルサイト 新まつもと物語 特集：神社仏閣巡り 須々岐水神社例大祭『お船祭り』] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 八十二文化財団 信州の文化財 里山辺須々岐水神社の御柱祭り] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すすきかわしんしや まつもとし}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:松本市の建築物]] [[Category:松本市の歴史]] [[Category:国史見在社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 須須岐水神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|250px]]
[[お船祭り (須々岐水神社)|お船祭り]]（[[2008年]][[5月5日]]） |所在地 = [[長野県]][[松本市]][[大字]][[里山辺村|里山辺]]2737長野県神社庁「[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm 神社紹介 松塩筑支部]」より（2016年1月24日閲覧）。座標は[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ ジオロケーター 日本語版]にて「須々岐水神社」をキーワードに検索して得た（2016年1月24日閲覧）。 | 緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 14 |緯度秒 = 0.94 | 経度度 = 138|経度分 = 0 |経度秒 = 27.6 |ラベル位置 = |地図 = Japan Nagano |祭神 = [[薄川]]の神・[[タケミナカタ|建御名方命]]・[[スサノオ|素盞鳴命]]信州・長野県観光協会「[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html 長野県公式観光ウェブサイト さわやか信州旅.net 須々岐水神社]」より（2016年1月24日閲覧）。 |神体 = |社格 = [[国史見在社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 須々岐水神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[5月5日]]（[[お船祭り (須々岐水神社)|お船祭り]]） |神事 = }} [[ファイル:Susuki River from Tsukamabashi Bridge.jpg|thumb|松本市を流れる[[薄川]]]] '''須々岐水神社'''（すすきがわじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[松本市]][[里山辺村|里山辺]]にある[[神社]]。[[登記]]上の[[宗教法人]]名称は'''須須岐水神社'''。 == 歴史 == * [[867年]]（[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]9年）に、[[大野川 (松本市)|安曇大野川]]の[[梓水神社]]とともに、[[正六位上]]から[[従五位下]]に昇叙されたと、『[[日本三代実録]]』に記録が残る。 * [[1458年]]（[[長禄]]2年）の墨書銘をもつ木造の[[狛犬]]が残る。 * 『[[信府統記]]』の「松本領諸社記」には、「薄宮大明神」として「山奥の大明神平に降りた神が、笹の葉に乗って[[薄川]]をくだり薄畑に着き、現在の社地に移った」との伝承が記録されている。 == 境内 == 本殿の建築年代は明らかになっていないが、延宝8年（1680年）以降の棟札が多く残っている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) お船祭りにみる歴史的風致|publisher=松本市|accessdate=2022-09-10}}。 [[境内]]の「桜之宮」には[[聖徳太子]]が祀られている。 == 例祭 == ;お船祭り :{{Main|お船祭り (須々岐水神社)}} :5月5日に行われる例祭である。9つの町会がもっている「お船」（一般には[[山車]]（だし）と呼ばれ、曳き山・屋台・舞台などと呼称することもあるが、須々岐水神社では「お船」と呼ぶ）を境内まで曳く。9基のお船には彫刻が施されており、いずれも長野県宝に指定されている。 ;御柱祭り :7年に1回、[[卯]]年と[[酉]]年に行われる。松本市の重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている。 == 交通アクセス == ;[[公共交通機関]] :[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[松本駅]]から[[タクシー]]で20[[分]]間。 ;[[自家用自動車]] :[[長野自動車道]]・[[松本インターチェンジ]]から7[[キロメートル]]、[[自動車]]で25分間。[[駐車場]]はない。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『松本まるごと博物館 ガイドブック』[[松本市立博物館]]、2008年3月 == 関連項目 == * [[須須岐水神社]]・[[お船祭り]] - 曖昧さ回避 * [[薄川]] * [[水神]] * [[里山辺村]] * [[御柱祭]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html 長野県公式観光ウェブサイト さわやか信州旅.net 須々岐水神社] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm 松本市公式観光情報ポータルサイト 新まつもと物語 特集：神社仏閣巡り 須々岐水神社例大祭『お船祭り』] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 八十二文化財団 信州の文化財 里山辺須々岐水神社の御柱祭り] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すすきかわしんしや まつもとし}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:松本市の建築物]] [[Category:松本市の歴史]] [[Category:国史見在社]]

[[お船祭り (須々岐水神社)|お船祭り]]（[[2008年]][[5月5日]]） |所在地 = [[長野県]][[松本市]][[大字]][[里山辺村|里山辺]]2737長野県神社庁「[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm 神社紹介 松塩筑支部]」より（2016年1月24日閲覧）。座標は[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ ジオロケーター 日本語版]にて「須々岐水神社」をキーワードに検索して得た（2016年1月24日閲覧）。 | 緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 14 |緯度秒 = 0.94 | 経度度 = 138|経度分 = 0 |経度秒 = 27.6 |ラベル位置 = |地図 = Japan Nagano |祭神 = [[薄川]]の神・[[タケミナカタ|建御名方命]]・[[スサノオ|素盞鳴命]]信州・長野県観光協会「[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html 長野県公式観光ウェブサイト さわやか信州旅.net 須々岐水神社]」より（2016年1月24日閲覧）。 |神体 = |社格 = [[国史見在社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 須々岐水神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[5月5日]]（[[お船祭り (須々岐水神社)|お船祭り]]） |神事 = }} [[ファイル:Susuki River from Tsukamabashi Bridge.jpg|thumb|松本市を流れる[[薄川]]]] '''須々岐水神社'''（すすきがわじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[松本市]][[里山辺村|里山辺]]にある[[神社]]。[[登記]]上の[[宗教法人]]名称は'''須須岐水神社'''。 == 歴史 == * 「[[日本後紀]]」延暦18年（799年）12月甲戌条に、[[高句麗]]から渡来した信濃国人卦婁真老（外従六位下）は「須々岐」の姓を与えられたとある。 * [[867年]]（[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]9年）に、[[大野川 (松本市)|安曇大野川]]の[[梓水神社]]とともに、[[正六位上]]から[[従五位下]]に昇叙されたと、『[[日本三代実録]]』に記録が残る。 * [[1458年]]（[[長禄]]2年）の墨書銘をもつ木造の[[狛犬]]が残る。 * 『[[信府統記]]』の「松本領諸社記」には、「薄宮大明神」として「山奥の大明神平に降りた神が、笹の葉に乗って[[薄川]]をくだり薄畑に着き、現在の社地に移った」との伝承が記録されている。 == 境内 == 本殿の建築年代は明らかになっていないが、延宝8年（1680年）以降の棟札が多く残っている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) お船祭りにみる歴史的風致|publisher=松本市|accessdate=2022-09-10}}。 [[境内]]の「桜之宮」には[[聖徳太子]]が祀られている。 == 例祭 == ;お船祭り :{{Main|お船祭り (須々岐水神社)}} :5月5日に行われる例祭である。9つの町会がもっている「お船」（一般には[[山車]]（だし）と呼ばれ、曳き山・屋台・舞台などと呼称することもあるが、須々岐水神社では「お船」と呼ぶ）を境内まで曳く。9基のお船には彫刻が施されており、いずれも長野県宝に指定されている。 ;御柱祭り :7年に1回、[[卯]]年と[[酉]]年に行われる。松本市の重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている。 == 交通アクセス == ;[[公共交通機関]] :[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[松本駅]]から[[タクシー]]で20[[分]]間。 ;[[自家用自動車]] :[[長野自動車道]]・[[松本インターチェンジ]]から7[[キロメートル]]、[[自動車]]で25分間。[[駐車場]]はない。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『松本まるごと博物館 ガイドブック』[[松本市立博物館]]、2008年3月 == 関連項目 == * [[須須岐水神社]]・[[お船祭り]] - 曖昧さ回避 * [[薄川]] * [[水神]] * [[里山辺村]] * [[御柱祭]] * [[松本市#名所・旧跡|松本市の名所・旧跡]] * [[松本市の歴史]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html 長野県公式観光ウェブサイト さわやか信州旅.net 須々岐水神社] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm 松本市公式観光情報ポータルサイト 新まつもと物語 特集：神社仏閣巡り 須々岐水神社例大祭『お船祭り』] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 八十二文化財団 信州の文化財 里山辺須々岐水神社の御柱祭り] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すすきかわしんしや まつもとし}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:松本市の建築物]] [[Category:松本市の歴史]] [[Category:国史見在社]]

revision.text:
[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] {{Shrine |Name = Susukigawa Shrine |Image = [[File:A ship energetically passing through a torii gate.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|en|お船祭り}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|en|里山辺村}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).Coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |Deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|en|薄川}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|en|タケミナカタ}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|en|素盞鳴命}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|en|国史見在社}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|en|5月5日}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|en|お船祭り}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|神社}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|en|里山辺村}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|en|日本後紀}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|en|高句麗}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|en|貞観}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|en|従五位下}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|en|梓水神社}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|日本三代実録}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|en|狛犬}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|en|長禄}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|en|信府統記}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|en|延宝}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|聖徳太子}}. == Annual Festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|en|5月5日}}. During the event, nine {{ill|decorated floats|en|山車}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|en|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|en|卯}} and {{ill|The Year of the Rooster|en|酉}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|en|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|en|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|en|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|en|須須岐水神社・お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|en|薄川}} * {{ill|Water Deities in Japan|en|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|en|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|en|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|en|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|en|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]]

During the event, nine {{ill|decorated floats|en|山車}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|en|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|en|卯}} and {{ill|The Year of the Rooster|en|酉}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|en|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|en|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|en|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|en|須須岐水神社・お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|en|薄川}} * {{ill|Water Deities in Japan|en|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|en|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|en|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|en|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|en|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]]

[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] {{Shrine |Name = Susukigawa Shrine |Image = [[File:A ship energetically passing through a torii gate.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|en|お船祭り}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|en|里山辺村}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).Coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |Deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|en|薄川}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|en|タケミナカタ}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|en|素盞鳴命}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|en|国史見在社}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|en|5月5日}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|en|お船祭り}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|神社}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|en|里山辺村}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|en|日本後紀}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|en|高句麗}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|en|貞観}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|en|従五位下}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|en|梓水神社}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|日本三代実録}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|en|狛犬}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|en|長禄}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|en|信府統記}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|en|延宝}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|聖徳太子}}. == Annual Festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|en|5月5日}}. During the event, nine {{ill|decorated floats|en|山車}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|en|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|en|卯}} and {{ill|The Year of the Rooster|en|酉}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|en|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|en|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|en|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|en|須須岐水神社・お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|en|薄川}} * {{ill|Water Deities in Japan|en|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|en|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|en|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|en|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|en|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]]

However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|en|須須岐水神社・お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|en|薄川}} * {{ill|Water Deities in Japan|en|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|en|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|en|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|en|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|en|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]]

[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] {{Shrine |Name = Susukigawa Shrine |image = [[File:A ship energetically passing through a torii gate.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|en|お船祭り}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|en|里山辺村}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).Coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|en|薄川}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|en|タケミナカタ}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|en|素盞鳴命}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|en|国史見在社}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|en|5月5日}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|en|お船祭り}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|神社}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|en|里山辺村}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|en|日本後紀}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|en|高句麗}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|en|貞観}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|en|従五位下}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|en|梓水神社}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|日本三代実録}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|en|狛犬}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|en|長禄}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|en|信府統記}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|en|延宝}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|聖徳太子}}. == Annual Festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|en|5月5日}}. During the event, nine {{ill|decorated floats|en|山車}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|en|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|en|卯}} and {{ill|The Year of the Rooster|en|酉}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|en|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|en|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|en|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|en|須須岐水神社・お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|en|薄川}} * {{ill|Water Deities in Japan|en|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|en|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|en|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|en|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|en|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]]

[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] {{Shrine |Name = Susukigawa Shrine |image = [[File:A ship energetically passing through a torii gate.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|en|お船祭り}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|en|里山辺村}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).Coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|en|薄川}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|en|タケミナカタ}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|en|素盞鳴命}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|en|国史見在社}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|en|5月5日}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|en|お船祭り}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|神社}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|en|里山辺村}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|en|日本後紀}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|en|高句麗}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|en|貞観}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|en|従五位下}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|en|梓水神社}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|日本三代実録}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|en|狛犬}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|en|長禄}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|en|信府統記}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|en|延宝}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|聖徳太子}}. == Annual Festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|en|5月5日}}. During the event, nine {{ill|decorated floats|en|山車}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|en|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|en|卯}} and {{ill|The Year of the Rooster|en|酉}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|en|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|en|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|en|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|en|須須岐水神社・お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|en|薄川}} * {{ill|Water Deities in Japan|en|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|en|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|en|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|en|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|en|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]]

[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] {{Shrine |Name = Susukigawa Shrine |image = [[File:A ship energetically passing through a torii gate.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|en|お船祭り}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|en|里山辺村}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).Coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|en|薄川}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|en|タケミナカタ}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|en|素盞鳴命}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|en|国史見在社}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|en|5月5日}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|en|お船祭り}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|神社}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|en|里山辺村}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|en|日本後紀}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|en|高句麗}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|en|貞観}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|en|従五位下}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|en|梓水神社}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|日本三代実録}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|en|狛犬}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|en|長禄}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|en|信府統記}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|en|延宝}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|聖徳太子}}. == Annual Festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|en|5月5日}}. During the event, nine {{ill|decorated floats|en|山車}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|en|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|en|卯}} and {{ill|The Year of the Rooster|en|酉}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|en|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|en|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|en|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|en|須須岐水神社・お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|en|薄川}} * {{ill|Water Deities in Japan|en|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|en|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|en|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|en|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|en|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:qq]]

{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).Coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. == Annual Festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}}. During the event, nine {{ill|decorated floats|ja|山車}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯}} and {{ill|The Year of the Rooster|ja|酉}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}} * {{ill|Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:qq]]

[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] {{Shrine |Name = Susukigawa Shrine |image = [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).Coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. == Annual Festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:qq]]

[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] {{Shrine |Name = Susukigawa Shrine |image = [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).Coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. == Annual Festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]]

{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]]

revision.text:
[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] {{Shrine |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]]

[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] {{Shrine |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)-qq]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]]

[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] {{Shrine |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)-qq]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]]
