However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)-qq]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)-qq]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ}}. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]]

[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]]

Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - revision.text:
[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)}}

{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)}}

These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)}} [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}}== See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)}} [[Category:New]]

[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]]

These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - [[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]
