However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社|12=draft}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|12=draft}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=draft}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|12=draft}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡|12=draft}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] [[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|12=draft}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=draft}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命|12=draft}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日|12=draft}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|12=draft}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=draft}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀|12=draft}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗|12=draft}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観|12=draft}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|12=draft}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬|12=draft}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄|12=draft}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記|12=draft}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝|12=draft}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日|12=draft}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats|12=draft}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|12=draft}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|12=draft}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster|12=draft}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関|12=draft}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|12=draft}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|12=draft}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社|12=draft}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|12=draft}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=draft}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|12=draft}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡|12=draft}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] [[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)}}

{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|12=draft}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=draft}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命|12=draft}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日|12=draft}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|12=draft}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=draft}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀|12=draft}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗|12=draft}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観|12=draft}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|12=draft}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬|12=draft}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄|12=draft}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記|12=draft}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝|12=draft}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日|12=draft}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats|12=draft}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|12=draft}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|12=draft}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster|12=draft}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関|12=draft}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|12=draft}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|12=draft}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社|12=draft}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|12=draft}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=draft}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|12=draft}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡|12=draft}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] }} [[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] [[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)}}

Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀|12=draft}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗|12=draft}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観|12=draft}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|12=draft}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬|12=draft}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄|12=draft}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記|12=draft}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝|12=draft}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日|12=draft}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats|12=draft}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|12=draft}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|12=draft}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster|12=draft}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関|12=draft}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|12=draft}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|12=draft}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社|12=draft}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|12=draft}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=draft}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|12=draft}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡|12=draft}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] [[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)}}

However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社|12=draft}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|12=draft}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=draft}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|12=draft}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡|12=draft}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] [[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|12=draft}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=draft}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命|12=draft}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日|12=draft}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|12=draft}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=draft}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀|12=draft}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗|12=draft}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観|12=draft}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|12=draft}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬|12=draft}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄|12=draft}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記|12=draft}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝|12=draft}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日|12=draft}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats|12=draft}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|12=draft}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|12=draft}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster|12=draft}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関|12=draft}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|12=draft}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|12=draft}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社|12=draft}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|12=draft}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|12=draft}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=draft}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|12=draft}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡|12=draft}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] [[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|12=draft|12=draft}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=draft|12=draft}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命|12=draft|12=draft}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft|12=draft}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|12=draft|12=draft}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=draft|12=draft}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀|12=draft|12=draft}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft|12=draft}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬|12=draft|12=draft}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄|12=draft|12=draft}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記|12=draft|12=draft}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝|12=draft|12=draft}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft|12=draft}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日|12=draft|12=draft}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats|12=draft|12=draft}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|12=draft|12=draft}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster|12=draft|12=draft}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関|12=draft|12=draft}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|12=draft|12=draft}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社|12=draft|12=draft}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] [[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|酉|" to "|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|2=ja|3=酉|4=fr|5=Coq (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Haan (astrologie)|lt=Rooster (酉)|"

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|2=ja|3=酉|4=fr|5=Coq (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Haan (astrologie)|lt=Rooster (酉)|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] [[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|2=ja|3=酉|4=fr|5=Coq (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Haan (astrologie)|lt=Rooster (酉)|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)}} {{translated page|ja|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|version=102333848|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] [[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]]

Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|2=ja|3=酉|4=fr|5=Coq (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Haan (astrologie)|lt=Rooster (酉)|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] {{translated page|ja|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|version=102333848|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Kokushi-ginzaisha]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] [[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]]

|LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usugawa River|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kōki|qq=}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goguryeo|qq=}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 9|qq=}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|qq=}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/komainu|qq=}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōroku 2|qq=}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinfu Tōki|qq=}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8|qq=}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōtoku|qq=}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 5|qq=}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuri float|qq=}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|qq=}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (Zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|qq=}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|2=ja|3=酉|4=fr|5=Coq (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Haan (astrologie)|lt=Rooster (酉)|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rooster (zodiac)|13=User:Immanuelle/Rooster (zodiac)|qq=}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Public Transportation|qq=}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Station|qq=}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|qq=}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Expressway|qq=}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|qq=}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susukigawa Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usugawa River|qq=}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin|qq=}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onbashira Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|qq=}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/History of Matsumoto City|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-ginzaisha|qq=}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 5|qq=}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kōki|qq=}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goguryeo|qq=}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Jōgan 9|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 9|qq=}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|qq=}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|komainu|ja|狛犬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/komainu|qq=}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōroku 2|qq=}}) with ink inscriptions. * The {{ill|Shinfu Tōki|ja|信府統記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinfu Tōki|qq=}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō 8|ja|延宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8|qq=}}) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōtoku|qq=}}. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 5|qq=}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|decorated floats=decorated floats|lt=decorated floats|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuri float|qq=}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|qq=}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (Zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|qq=}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|ja|酉|1=Rooster (Zodiac)|lt=Rooster (酉)|2=ja|3=酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|2=ja|3=酉|4=fr|5=Coq (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Haan (astrologie)|lt=Rooster (酉)|Rooster (zodiac)=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=The Year of the Rooster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rooster (zodiac)|13=User:Immanuelle/Rooster (zodiac)|qq=}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; {{ill|Public Transportation|ja|公共交通機関|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Public Transportation|qq=}} : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Station|qq=}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|qq=}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Expressway|qq=}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|qq=}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susukigawa Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usugawa River|qq=}} * {{ill|Suijin|lt=Water Deities in Japan|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin|qq=}} * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=}} * {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onbashira Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|qq=}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/History of Matsumoto City|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] {{translated page|ja|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|version=102333848|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] [[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]]

Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]] (799) mentions a man from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan 9]]), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|qq=}}, as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|komainu]] from 1458 ([[:en:Chōroku|Chōroku 2]]) with ink inscriptions. * The [[:en:Shimpu-tōki|Shinfu Tōki]] refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō 8]]) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on [[:en:May 5|May 5]]. During the event, nine [[:en:Matsuri float|decorated floats]], referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|qq=}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (Zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|qq=}} and [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|The Year of the Rooster]], this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; [[:en:Public transport|Public Transportation]] : From [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]], it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|qq=}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the [[:en:Nagano Expressway|Nagano Expressway]] {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|qq=}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susukigawa Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usugawa River|qq=}} * [[:en:Suijin|water god]] * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=}} * [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|qq=}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/History of Matsumoto City|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] {{translated page|ja|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|version=102333848|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] [[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]]

Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]] (799) mentions a man from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan 9]]), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|qq=}}, as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|komainu]] from 1458 ([[:en:Chōroku|Chōroku 2]]) with ink inscriptions. * The [[:en:Shimpu-tōki|Shinfu Tōki]] refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō 8]]) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on [[:en:May 5|May 5]]. During the event, nine [[:en:Matsuri float|decorated floats]], referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|qq=}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (Zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|qq=}} and [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|The Year of the Rooster]], this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; [[:en:Public transport|Public Transportation]] : From [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]], it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|qq=}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the [[:en:Nagano Expressway|Nagano Expressway]] {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|qq=}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susukigawa Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usugawa River|qq=}} * [[:en:Suijin|water god]] * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=}} * [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|qq=}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/History of Matsumoto City|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] {{translated page|ja|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|version=102333848|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] [[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[en:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:松本市の建築物]] [[Category:松本市の歴史]]
