{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usugawa River|qq=}}, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-ginzaisha|qq=}} |AnnualFestival = [[:en:May 5|May 5]] ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]] (799) mentions a man from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan 9]]), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|qq=}}, as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|komainu]] from 1458 ([[:en:Chōroku|Chōroku 2]]) with ink inscriptions. * The [[:en:Shimpu-tōki|Shinfu Tōki]] refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō 8]]) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on [[:en:May 5|May 5]]. During the event, nine [[:en:Matsuri float|decorated floats]], referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|qq=}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (Zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|qq=}} and [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|The Year of the Rooster]], this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; [[:en:Public transport|Public Transportation]] : From [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]], it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|qq=}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the [[:en:Nagano Expressway|Nagano Expressway]] {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|qq=}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susukigawa Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usugawa River|qq=}} * [[:en:Suijin|water god]] * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=}} * [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|qq=}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/History of Matsumoto City|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] {{translated page|ja|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|version=102333848|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] [[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsumoto]] [[Category:松本市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]]

However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susukigawa Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usugawa River|qq=}} * [[:en:Suijin|water god]] * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=}} * [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|qq=}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/History of Matsumoto City|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] {{translated page|ja|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|version=102333848|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] [[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto�]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usugawa River|qq=}}, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-ginzaisha|qq=}} |AnnualFestival = [[:en:May 5|May 5]] ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]] (799) mentions a man from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan 9]]), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|qq=}}, as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|komainu]] from 1458 ([[:en:Chōroku|Chōroku 2]]) with ink inscriptions. * The [[:en:Shimpu-tōki|Shinfu Tōki]] refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō 8]]) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on [[:en:May 5|May 5]]. During the event, nine [[:en:Matsuri float|decorated floats]], referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|qq=}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (Zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|qq=}} and [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|The Year of the Rooster]], this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; [[:en:Public transport|Public Transportation]] : From [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]], it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|qq=}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the [[:en:Nagano Expressway|Nagano Expressway]] {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|qq=}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susukigawa Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usugawa River|qq=}} * [[:en:Suijin|water god]] * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=}} * [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|qq=}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/History of Matsumoto City|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] {{translated page|ja|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|version=102333848|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] [[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]]

However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susukigawa Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usugawa River|qq=}} * [[:en:Suijin|water god]] * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=}} * [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|qq=}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/History of Matsumoto City|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] {{translated page|ja|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|version=102333848|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] [[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|250px]]
{{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} ([[May 5, 2008]]) |location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=}} 2737From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016). |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = God of {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usugawa River|qq=}}, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}Shinshu Nagano Tourism Association: "[http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Susukigawa Shrine on the Official Nagano Prefecture Tourism Website]" (accessed January 24, 2016). |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-ginzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |AnnualFestival = [[:en:May 5|May 5]] ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]] (799) mentions a man from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan 9]]), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|komainu]] from 1458 ([[:en:Chōroku|Chōroku 2]]) with ink inscriptions. * The [[:en:Shimpu-tōki|Shinfu Tōki]] refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō 8]]) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on [[:en:May 5|May 5]]. During the event, nine [[:en:Matsuri float|decorated floats]], referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (Zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|qq=}} and [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|The Year of the Rooster]], this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; [[:en:Public transport|Public Transportation]] : From [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]], it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|qq=}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the [[:en:Nagano Expressway|Nagano Expressway]] {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|qq=}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susukigawa Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usugawa River|qq=}} * [[:en:Suijin|water god]] * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=}} * [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|qq=}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本市の歴史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/History of Matsumoto City|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] {{translated page|ja|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|version=102333848|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] [[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]]

Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}) }} '''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals. == History == * The [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]] (799) mentions a man from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan 9]]), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|komainu]] from 1458 ([[:en:Chōroku|Chōroku 2]]) with ink inscriptions. * The [[:en:Shimpu-tōki|Shinfu Tōki]] refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site. == Grounds == The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō 8]]) have been preserved.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}} A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]. == festivals == ; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on [[:en:May 5|May 5]]. During the event, nine [[:en:Matsuri float|decorated floats]], referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano. ; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (Zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|qq=}} and [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|The Year of the Rooster]], this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City. == Transportation == ; [[:en:Public transport|Public Transportation]] : From [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]], it is a 20-minute taxi ride. ; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|qq=}} : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the [[:en:Nagano Expressway|Nagano Expressway]] {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|qq=}}. However, there is no designated parking area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also == * {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susukigawa Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usugawa River|qq=}} * [[:en:Suijin|water god]] * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=}} * [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|qq=}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本の Municipal History|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/History of Matsumoto City|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine] {{translated page|ja|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|version=102333848|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] [[sn:Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]]

'''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals.
== History ==
* The [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]] (799) mentions a man from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan 9]]), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|komainu]] from 1458 ([[:en:Chōroku|Chōroku 2]]) with ink inscriptions.<ref name="history" /> * The [[:en:Shimpu-tōki|Shinfu Tōki]] refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site.
== Grounds ==
The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō 8]]) have been preserved.<ref name="history">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}}</ref>
A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]].
== festivals ==
; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on [[:en:May 5|May 5]]. During the event, nine [[:en:Matsuri float|decorated floats]], referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano.
; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (Zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|qq=}} and [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|The Year of the Rooster]], this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City.
== Transportation ==
; [[:en:Public transport|Public Transportation]]<ref name="tabinet" /> : From [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]], it is a 20-minute taxi ride.
; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|qq=}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the [[:en:Nagano Expressway|Nagano Expressway]] {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|qq=}}. However, there is no designated parking area.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11664481]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[:en:Matsumoto|Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=}} 2737<ref>From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).</ref><ref>coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016).</ref> |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Susuki River|qid=Q11622154}} |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-ginzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |AnnualFestival = [[:en:May 5|May 5]] ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}) |location = {{ill|Matsumoto|qid=Q213324}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.233583333333335|N|138.00766666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals.
== History ==
* The [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]] (799) mentions a man from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan 9]]), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|komainu]] from 1458 ([[:en:Chōroku|Chōroku 2]]) with ink inscriptions.<ref name="history" /> * The [[:en:Shimpu-tōki|Shinfu Tōki]] refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site.
== Grounds ==
The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō 8]]) have been preserved.<ref name="history">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}}</ref>
A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]].
== festivals ==
; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on [[:en:May 5|May 5]]. During the event, nine [[:en:Matsuri float|decorated floats]], referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano.
; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (Zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|qq=}} and [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|The Year of the Rooster]], this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City.
== Transportation ==
; [[:en:Public transport|Public Transportation]]<ref name="tabinet" /> : From [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]], it is a 20-minute taxi ride.
; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|qq=}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the [[:en:Nagano Expressway|Nagano Expressway]] {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|qq=}}. However, there is no designated parking area.

; [[:en:Public transport|Public Transportation]]<ref name="tabinet" /> : From [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]], it is a 20-minute taxi ride.
; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|qq=|1=Owner-driver car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133282087}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the [[:en:Nagano Expressway|Nagano Expressway]] {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|qq=|1=Matsumoto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11104486}}. However, there is no designated parking area.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11664481]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[:en:Matsumoto|Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}} 2737<ref>From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).</ref><ref>coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016).</ref> |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Susuki River|qid=Q11622154}} |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-ginzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} |AnnualFestival = [[:en:May 5|May 5]] ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}) |location = {{ill|Matsumoto|qid=Q213324}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.233583333333335|N|138.00766666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals.
== History ==
* The [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]] (799) mentions a man from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan 9]]), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|komainu]] from 1458 ([[:en:Chōroku|Chōroku 2]]) with ink inscriptions.<ref name="history" /> * The [[:en:Shimpu-tōki|Shinfu Tōki]] refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site.
== Grounds ==
The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō 8]]) have been preserved.<ref name="history">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}}</ref>
A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]].
== festivals ==
; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on [[:en:May 5|May 5]]. During the event, nine [[:en:Matsuri float|decorated floats]], referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano.
; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=mǎo|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16662792}} and [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|The Year of the Rooster]], this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City.
== Transportation ==
; [[:en:Public transport|Public Transportation]]<ref name="tabinet" /> : From [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]], it is a 20-minute taxi ride.
; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|qq=|1=Owner-driver car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133282087}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the [[:en:Nagano Expressway|Nagano Expressway]] {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|qq=|1=Matsumoto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11104486}}. However, there is no designated parking area.

Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals.
== History ==
* The [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]] (799) mentions a man from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan 9]]), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|komainu]] from 1458 ([[:en:Chōroku|Chōroku 2]]) with ink inscriptions.<ref name="history" /> * The [[:en:Shimpu-tōki|Shinfu Tōki]] refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site.
== Grounds ==
The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō 8]]) have been preserved.<ref name="history">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}}</ref>
A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]].
== festivals ==
; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on [[:en:May 5|May 5]]. During the event, nine [[:en:Matsuri float|decorated floats]], referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano.
; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=mǎo|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16662792}} and [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|The Year of the Rooster]], this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City.
== Transportation ==
; [[:en:Public transport|Public Transportation]]<ref name="tabinet" /> : From [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]], it is a 20-minute taxi ride.
; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|qq=|1=Owner-driver car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133282087}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the [[:en:Nagano Expressway|Nagano Expressway]] {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|qq=|1=Matsumoto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11104486}}. However, there is no designated parking area.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
* *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also ==
* {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susukigawa Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usugawa River|qq=|1=Susuki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622154}} * [[:en:Suijin|water god]] * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}} * [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|qq=|1=Matsumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q213324}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本の Municipal History|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/History of Matsumoto City|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11664481]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[:en:Matsumoto|Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}} 2737<ref>From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).</ref><ref>coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016).</ref> |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Susuki River|qid=Q11622154}} |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-ginzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} |AnnualFestival = [[:en:May 5|May 5]] ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}) |location = {{ill|Matsumoto|qid=Q213324}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.233583333333335|N|138.00766666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals.
== History ==
* The [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]] (799) mentions a man from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan 9]]), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|komainu]] from 1458 ([[:en:Chōroku|Chōroku 2]]) with ink inscriptions.<ref name="history" /> * The [[:en:Shimpu-tōki|Shinfu Tōki]] refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site.
== Grounds ==
The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō 8]]) have been preserved.<ref name="history">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}}</ref>
A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]].
== festivals ==
; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on [[:en:May 5|May 5]]. During the event, nine [[:en:Matsuri float|decorated floats]], referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano.
; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=mǎo|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16662792}} and [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|The Year of the Rooster]], this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City.
== Transportation ==
; [[:en:Public transport|Public Transportation]]<ref name="tabinet" /> : From [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]], it is a 20-minute taxi ride.
; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|qq=|1=Owner-driver car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133282087}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the [[:en:Nagano Expressway|Nagano Expressway]] {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|qq=|1=Matsumoto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11104486}}. However, there is no designated parking area.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
* *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also ==
* {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine|dd=Susukigawa Shrine and Funamatsuri Festival|ja|須須岐水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susukigawa Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Usugawa River|ja|薄川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usugawa River|qq=|1=Susuki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622154}} * [[:en:Suijin|water god]] * {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}} * [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] * {{ill|Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|ja|松本市#名所・旧跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Notable Sites in Matsumoto City|qq=|1=Matsumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q213324}} * {{ill|History of Matsumoto City|ja|松本の Municipal History|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/History of Matsumoto City|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.nagano-tabi.net/modules/enjoy/enjoy_2023006.html Nagano Prefecture Official Tourism Website - Susukigawa Shrine] * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1_index-id_78-htm Matsumoto City Official Portal - Susukigawa Shrine Funamatsuri Festival] * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4634&seq=0 82 Cultural Foundation - Onbashira Festival at Susukigawa Shrine]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11664481]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[:en:Matsumoto|Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}} 2737<ref>From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).</ref><ref>coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016).</ref> |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Susuki River|WD=Q11622154}} |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-ginzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} |AnnualFestival = [[:en:May 5|May 5]] ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}) |location = {{ill|Matsumoto|WD=Q213324}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.233583333333335|N|138.00766666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|qq=|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals.
== History ==
* The [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]] (799) mentions a man from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan 9]]), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|komainu]] from 1458 ([[:en:Chōroku|Chōroku 2]]) with ink inscriptions.<ref name="history" /> * The [[:en:Shimpu-tōki|Shinfu Tōki]] refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site.
== Grounds ==
The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō 8]]) have been preserved.<ref name="history">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}}</ref>
A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]].
== festivals ==
; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on [[:en:May 5|May 5]]. During the event, nine [[:en:Matsuri float|decorated floats]], referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano.
; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=mǎo|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16662792}} and [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|The Year of the Rooster]], this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City.
== Transportation ==
; [[:en:Public transport|Public Transportation]]<ref name="tabinet" /> : From [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]], it is a 20-minute taxi ride.
; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|qq=|1=Owner-driver car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133282087}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the [[:en:Nagano Expressway|Nagano Expressway]] {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|qq=|1=Matsumoto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11104486}}. However, there is no designated parking area.

; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=mǎo|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16662792}} and [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|The Year of the Rooster]], this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City.
== Transportation ==
; [[:en:Public transport|Public Transportation]]<ref name="tabinet" /> : From [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]], it is a 20-minute taxi ride.
; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|1=Owner-driver car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133282087}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the [[:en:Nagano Expressway|Nagano Expressway]] {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|1=Matsumoto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11104486}}. However, there is no designated parking area.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11664481]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[:en:Matsumoto|Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}} 2737<ref>From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).</ref><ref>coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016).</ref> |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Susuki River|WD=Q11622154}} |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} |AnnualFestival = [[:en:May 5|May 5]] ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}) |location = {{ill|Matsumoto|WD=Q213324}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.233583333333335|N|138.00766666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals.
== History ==
* The [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]] (799) mentions a man from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan 9]]), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|komainu]] from 1458 ([[:en:Chōroku|Chōroku 2]]) with ink inscriptions.<ref name="history" /> * The [[:en:Shimpu-tōki|Shinfu Tōki]] refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site.
== Grounds ==
The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō 8]]) have been preserved.<ref name="history">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}}</ref>
A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]].
== festivals ==
; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on [[:en:May 5|May 5]]. During the event, nine [[:en:Matsuri float|decorated floats]], referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano.
; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=mǎo|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16662792}} and [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|The Year of the Rooster]], this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City.
== Transportation ==
; [[:en:Public transport|Public Transportation]]<ref name="tabinet" /> : From [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]], it is a 20-minute taxi ride.
; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|1=Owner-driver car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133282087}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the [[:en:Nagano Expressway|Nagano Expressway]] {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|1=Matsumoto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11104486}}. However, there is no designated parking area.

The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō 8]]) have been preserved.<ref name="history">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}}</ref>
A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]].
== festivals ==
; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on [[:en:May 5|May 5]]. During the event, nine [[:en:Matsuri float|decorated floats]], referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano.
; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=mǎo|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16662792}} and [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|The Year of the Rooster]], this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City.
== Transportation ==
; [[:en:Public transport|Public Transportation]]<ref name="tabinet" /> : From [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]], it is a 20-minute taxi ride.
; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|1=Owner-driver car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133282087}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the [[:en:Nagano Expressway|Nagano Expressway]] {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|1=Matsumoto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11104486}}. However, there is no designated parking area.

'''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals.
== History ==
* The [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]] (799) mentions a man from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan 9]]), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|komainu]] from 1458 ([[:en:Chōroku|Chōroku 2]]) with ink inscriptions.<ref name="history" /> * The [[:en:Shimpu-tōki|Shinfu Tōki]] refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site.
== Grounds ==
The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō 8]]) have been preserved.<ref name="history">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}}</ref>
A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]].
== festivals ==
; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on [[:en:May 5|May 5]]. During the event, nine [[:en:Matsuri float|decorated floats]], referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano.
; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=mǎo|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16662792}} and [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|The Year of the Rooster]], this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City.
== Transportation ==
; [[:en:Public transport|Public Transportation]]<ref name="tabinet" /> : From [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]], it is a 20-minute taxi ride.
; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|1=Owner-driver car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133282087}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the [[:en:Nagano Expressway|Nagano Expressway]] {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|1=Matsumoto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11104486}}. However, there is no designated parking area.

'''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals.
== History ==
* The [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]] (799) mentions a man from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan 9]]), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|komainu]] from 1458 ([[:en:Chōroku|Chōroku 2]]) with ink inscriptions.<ref name="history" /> * The [[:en:Shimpu-tōki|Shinfu Tōki]] refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site.
== Grounds ==
The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō 8]]) have been preserved.<ref name="history">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}}</ref>
A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]].
== festivals ==
; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on [[:en:May 5|May 5]]. During the event, nine [[:en:Matsuri float|decorated floats]], referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano.
; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=mǎo|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16662792}} and [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|The Year of the Rooster]], this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City.
== Transportation ==
; [[:en:Public transport|Public Transportation]]<ref name="tabinet" /> : From [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]], it is a 20-minute taxi ride.
; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|1=Owner-driver car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133282087}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the [[:en:Nagano Expressway|Nagano Expressway]] {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|1=Matsumoto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11104486}}. However, there is no designated parking area.
