<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11664481]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[:en:Matsumoto|Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}} 2737<ref>From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).</ref><ref>coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016).</ref> |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Susuki River|WD=Q11622154}} |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} |AnnualFestival = [[:en:May 5|May 5]] ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}) |location = {{ill|Matsumoto|WD=Q213324}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.233583333333335|N|138.00766666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals.
== History ==
* The [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]] (799) mentions a man from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan 9]]), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|komainu]] from 1458 ([[:en:Chōroku|Chōroku 2]]) with ink inscriptions.<ref name="history" /> * The [[:en:Shimpu-tōki|Shinfu Tōki]] refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site.
== Grounds ==
The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō 8]]) have been preserved.<ref name="history">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}}</ref>
A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]].
== festivals ==
; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on [[:en:May 5|May 5]]. During the event, nine [[:en:Matsuri float|decorated floats]], referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano.
; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=mǎo|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16662792}} and [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|The Year of the Rooster]], this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City.
== Transportation ==
; [[:en:Public transport|Public Transportation]]<ref name="tabinet" /> : From [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]], it is a 20-minute taxi ride.
; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|1=Owner-driver car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133282087}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the [[:en:Nagano Expressway|Nagano Expressway]] {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|1=Matsumoto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11104486}}. However, there is no designated parking area.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
* *{{Cite book|title=Complete Guide to the Matsumoto Museum|publisher=Matsumoto City Museum|date=March 2008}} == See Also ==

== External Links ==

; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|1=Owner-driver car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133282087}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the [[:en:Nagano Expressway|Nagano Expressway]] {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|1=Matsumoto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11104486}}. However, there is no designated parking area.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11664481]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[:en:Matsumoto|Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}} 2737<ref>From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).</ref><ref>coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016).</ref> |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Susuki River|WD=Q11622154}} |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} |AnnualFestival = [[:en:May 5|May 5]] ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}) |location = {{ill|Matsumoto|WD=Q213324}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.233583333333335|N|138.00766666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals.
== History ==
* The [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]] (799) mentions a man from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan 9]]), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|komainu]] from 1458 ([[:en:Chōroku|Chōroku 2]]) with ink inscriptions.<ref name="history" /> * The [[:en:Shimpu-tōki|Shinfu Tōki]] refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site.
== Grounds ==
The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō 8]]) have been preserved.<ref name="history">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}}</ref>
A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]].
== festivals ==
; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on [[:en:May 5|May 5]]. During the event, nine [[:en:Matsuri float|decorated floats]], referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano.
; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=mǎo|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16662792}} and [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|The Year of the Rooster]], this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City.
== Transportation ==
; [[:en:Public transport|Public Transportation]]<ref name="tabinet" /> : From [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]], it is a 20-minute taxi ride.
; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|1=Owner-driver car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133282087}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the [[:en:Nagano Expressway|Nagano Expressway]] {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|1=Matsumoto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11104486}}. However, there is no designated parking area.

These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano.
; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=mǎo|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16662792}} and [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|The Year of the Rooster]], this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City.
== Transportation ==
; [[:en:Public transport|Public Transportation]]<ref name="tabinet" /> : From [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]], it is a 20-minute taxi ride.
; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|1=Owner-driver car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133282087}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the [[:en:Nagano Expressway|Nagano Expressway]] {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|1=Matsumoto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11104486}}. However, there is no designated parking area.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:須々岐水神社 (松本市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11664481]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[:en:Matsumoto|Matsumoto]], {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}} 2737<ref>From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).</ref><ref>coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016).</ref> |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Susuki River|WD=Q11622154}} |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} |AnnualFestival = [[:en:May 5|May 5]] ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}) |location = {{ill|Matsumoto|WD=Q213324}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.233583333333335|N|138.00766666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals.
== History ==
* The [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]] (799) mentions a man from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan 9]]), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|komainu]] from 1458 ([[:en:Chōroku|Chōroku 2]]) with ink inscriptions.<ref name="history" /> * The [[:en:Shimpu-tōki|Shinfu Tōki]] refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site.
== Grounds ==
The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō 8]]) have been preserved.<ref name="history">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}}</ref>
A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]].
== festivals ==
; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on [[:en:May 5|May 5]]. During the event, nine [[:en:Matsuri float|decorated floats]], referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano.
; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=mǎo|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16662792}} and [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|The Year of the Rooster]], this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City.
== Transportation ==
; [[:en:Public transport|Public Transportation]]<ref name="tabinet" /> : From [[:en:Matsumoto Station|Matsumoto Station]], it is a 20-minute taxi ride.
; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|1=Owner-driver car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133282087}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the [[:en:Nagano Expressway|Nagano Expressway]] {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|1=Matsumoto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11104486}}. However, there is no designated parking area.

'''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals.
== History ==
* The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|en|Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1077029|lt=Nihon Kōki}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan 9}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|WD=Q20155846|lt=komainu}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku|en|Chōroku|WD=Q1193524|lt=Chōroku 2}}) with ink inscriptions.<ref name="history" /> * The {{ill|Shimpu-tōki|en|Shimpu-tōki|WD=Q11386122|lt=Shinfu Tōki}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site.
== Grounds ==
The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō 8}}) have been preserved.<ref name="history">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}}</ref>
A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku}}.
== festivals ==
; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|en|May 5|WD=Q2550|lt=May 5}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=decorated floats}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano.
; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=mǎo|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16662792}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|en|Rooster (zodiac)|WD=Q822621|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City.
== Transportation ==
; {{ill|Public transport|en|Public transport|WD=Q178512|lt=Public Transportation}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|Matsumoto Station|WD=Q843167|lt=Matsumoto Station}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride.
; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|1=Owner-driver car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133282087}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|Nagano Expressway|WD=Q6958441|lt=Nagano Expressway}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|1=Matsumoto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11104486}}. However, there is no designated parking area.

'''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals.
== History ==
* The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|en|Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1077029|lt=Nihon Kōki}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan 9}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|WD=Q20155846|lt=komainu}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku|en|Chōroku|WD=Q1193524|lt=Chōroku 2}}) with ink inscriptions.<ref name="history" /> * The {{ill|Shimpu-tōki|en|Shimpu-tōki|WD=Q11386122|lt=Shinfu Tōki}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site.
== Grounds ==
The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō 8}}) have been preserved.<ref name="history">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}}</ref>
A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku}}.
== festivals ==
; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|en|May 5|WD=Q2550|lt=May 5}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=decorated floats}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano.
; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=mǎo|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16662792}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|en|Rooster (zodiac)|WD=Q822621|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City.
== Transportation ==
; {{ill|Public transport|en|Public transport|WD=Q178512|lt=Public Transportation}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|Matsumoto Station|WD=Q843167|lt=Matsumoto Station}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride.
; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|1=Owner-driver car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133282087}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|Nagano Expressway|WD=Q6958441|lt=Nagano Expressway}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|1=Matsumoto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11104486}}. However, there is no designated parking area.

; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=mǎo|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16662792}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|en|Rooster (zodiac)|WD=Q822621|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City.

; {{ill|Public transport|en|Public transport|WD=Q178512|lt=Public Transportation}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|Matsumoto Station|WD=Q843167|lt=Matsumoto Station}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride.
; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|1=Owner-driver car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133282087}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|Nagano Expressway|WD=Q6958441|lt=Nagano Expressway}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|1=Matsumoto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11104486}}. However, there is no designated parking area.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano}}, {{ill|Matsumoto|en|Matsumoto|WD=Q256944|lt=Matsumoto}}, {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}} 2737<ref>From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).</ref><ref>coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016).</ref> |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Susuki River|WD=Q11622154}} |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|en|May 5|WD=Q2550|lt=May 5}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}) |location = {{ill|Matsumoto|WD=Q213324}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.233583333333335|N|138.00766666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals.
== History ==
* The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|en|Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1077029|lt=Nihon Kōki}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan 9}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|WD=Q20155846|lt=komainu}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku|en|Chōroku|WD=Q1193524|lt=Chōroku 2}}) with ink inscriptions.<ref name="history" /> * The {{ill|Shimpu-tōki|en|Shimpu-tōki|WD=Q11386122|lt=Shinfu Tōki}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site.
== Grounds ==
The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō 8}}) have been preserved.<ref name="history">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}}</ref>
A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku}}.
== festivals ==
; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|en|May 5|WD=Q2550|lt=May 5}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=decorated floats}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano.
; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=mǎo|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16662792}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|en|Rooster (zodiac)|WD=Q822621|lt=The Year of the Rooster}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City.
== Transportation ==
; {{ill|Public transport|en|Public transport|WD=Q178512|lt=Public Transportation}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|Matsumoto Station|WD=Q843167|lt=Matsumoto Station}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride.
; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|1=Owner-driver car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133282087}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|Nagano Expressway|WD=Q6958441|lt=Nagano Expressway}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|1=Matsumoto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11104486}}. However, there is no designated parking area.

{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsumoto|en|Matsumoto|WD=Q256944|lt=Matsumoto|1=Matsumoto}}, {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}} 2737<ref>From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).</ref><ref>coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016).</ref> |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Susuki River|WD=Q11622154|1=Susuki River}} |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|en|May 5|WD=Q2550|lt=May 5|1=May 5}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}) |location = {{ill|Matsumoto|WD=Q213324|1=Matsumoto, Nagano}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.233583333333335|N|138.00766666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals.
== History ==
* The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|en|Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1077029|lt=Nihon Kōki|1=Nihon Kōki}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo|1=Goguryeo}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan 9|1=Zhenguan}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|WD=Q20155846|lt=komainu|1=Komainu}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku|en|Chōroku|WD=Q1193524|lt=Chōroku 2|1=Chōroku}}) with ink inscriptions.<ref name="history" /> * The {{ill|Shimpu-tōki|en|Shimpu-tōki|WD=Q11386122|lt=Shinfu Tōki|1=Shimpu-tōki}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site.
== Grounds ==
The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō 8|1=Enpō}}) have been preserved.<ref name="history">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}}</ref>
A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}}.
== festivals ==
; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|en|May 5|WD=Q2550|lt=May 5|1=May 5}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=decorated floats|1=Matsuri float}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano.
; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=mǎo|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16662792}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|en|Rooster (zodiac)|WD=Q822621|lt=The Year of the Rooster|1=Rooster (zodiac)}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City.
== Transportation ==
; {{ill|Public transport|en|Public transport|WD=Q178512|lt=Public Transportation|1=Public transport}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|Matsumoto Station|WD=Q843167|lt=Matsumoto Station|1=Matsumoto Station}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride.
; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|1=Owner-driver car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133282087}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|Nagano Expressway|WD=Q6958441|lt=Nagano Expressway|1=Nagano Expressway}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|1=Matsumoto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11104486}}. However, there is no designated parking area.

== festivals ==
; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|en|May 5|WD=Q2550|lt=May 5|1=May 5}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=decorated floats|1=Matsuri float}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano.
; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=mǎo|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16662792}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|en|Rooster (zodiac)|WD=Q822621|lt=The Year of the Rooster|1=Rooster (zodiac)}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City.
== Transportation ==
; {{ill|Public transport|en|Public transport|WD=Q178512|lt=Public Transportation|1=Public transport}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|Matsumoto Station|WD=Q843167|lt=Matsumoto Station|1=Matsumoto Station}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride.
; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|1=Owner-driver car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133282087}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|Nagano Expressway|WD=Q6958441|lt=Nagano Expressway|1=Nagano Expressway}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|1=Matsumoto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11104486}}. However, there is no designated parking area.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susukigawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsumoto|en|Matsumoto|WD=Q256944|lt=Matsumoto|1=Matsumoto}}, {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}} 2737<ref>From Nagano Shrine Association's "[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm Shrine Introductions, Matsushiochi District]" (accessed January 24, 2016).</ref><ref>coordinates obtained using "[http://wikitools.web.fc2.com/geo/ Geolocator, Japanese version]" with the keyword "Susukigawa Shrine" (accessed January 24, 2016).</ref> |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 14 |LatitudeSeconds = 0.94 |LongitudeDegrees = 138 |LongitudeMinutes = 0 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.6 |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Susuki River|WD=Q11622154|1=Susuki River}} |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-ginzaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} |AnnualFestival = {{ill|May 5|en|May 5|WD=Q2550|lt=May 5|1=May 5}} ({{ill|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}) |location = {{ill|Matsumoto|WD=Q213324|1=Matsumoto, Nagano}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.233583333333335|N|138.00766666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Susukigawa Shrine''' (須々岐水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Satoyamabe Village|ja|里山辺村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satoyamabe Village|1=Satoyamabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644586}}, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. Officially registered under the religious corporation system as "Susukigawa Shrine," it is known for its historical significance and annual rituals.
== History ==
* The {{ill|Nihon Kōki|en|Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1077029|lt=Nihon Kōki|1=Nihon Kōki}} (799) mentions a man from {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo|1=Goguryeo}}, Kero Mano (外従六位下), who was given the surname "Susuki" after settling in Shinano Province. * In 867 ({{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan 9|1=Zhenguan}}), it was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, along with {{ill|Azusamizu Shrine|ja|梓水神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azusamizu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}. * The shrine houses a pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|WD=Q20155846|lt=komainu|1=Komainu}} from 1458 ({{ill|Chōroku|en|Chōroku|WD=Q1193524|lt=Chōroku 2|1=Chōroku}}) with ink inscriptions.<ref name="history" /> * The {{ill|Shimpu-tōki|en|Shimpu-tōki|WD=Q11386122|lt=Shinfu Tōki|1=Shimpu-tōki}} refers to the shrine as "Usunomiya Daimyōjin," documenting a legend where a deity descended the Usugawa River on bamboo leaves and established itself at the current site.
== Grounds ==
The construction date of the main hall remains unclear, but numerous plaques dating back to 1680 ({{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō 8|1=Enpō}}) have been preserved.<ref name="history">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20343.pdf|title=(5) Historical Significance Seen Through the Funamatsuri Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=September 10, 2022}}</ref>
A sacred area known as "Sakura-no-Miya" within the precincts enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}}.
== festivals ==
; The Funamatsuri Festival :{{Main|The Funamatsuri Festival (Susukigawa Shrine)}} :The festival dates back to the Edo period and is held annually on {{ill|May 5|en|May 5|WD=Q2550|lt=May 5|1=May 5}}. During the event, nine {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=decorated floats|1=Matsuri float}}, referred to as "Ofune" (ships) at this shrine, are paraded to the precincts. These floats, adorned with intricate carvings, are designated as {{ill|Prefectural Treasures|ja|県宝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Treasures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano.
; Onbashira Festival : Held every seven years in {{ill|The Year of the Rabbit|ja|卯|1=mǎo|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Year of the Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16662792}} and {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|en|Rooster (zodiac)|WD=Q822621|lt=The Year of the Rooster|1=Rooster (zodiac)}}, this festival is recognized as a significant intangible cultural property of Matsumoto City.
== Transportation ==
; {{ill|Public transport|en|Public transport|WD=Q178512|lt=Public Transportation|1=Public transport}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : From {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|Matsumoto Station|WD=Q843167|lt=Matsumoto Station|1=Matsumoto Station}}, it is a 20-minute taxi ride.
; {{ill|Private Vehicles|ja|自家用自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Private Vehicles|1=Owner-driver car|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133282087}}<ref name="tabinet" /> : The shrine is located 7 kilometers from the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|Nagano Expressway|WD=Q6958441|lt=Nagano Expressway|1=Nagano Expressway}} {{ill|Matsumoto Interchange|ja|松本インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Interchange|1=Matsumoto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11104486}}. However, there is no designated parking area.
